_comment: Essential medicines available at a Sub Health Centre upgraded to Health and Wellness Centre (SHC-HWC) in rural West Bengal. Source: Ayushman Bharat Operational Guidelines for Health and Wellness Centers (MoHFW, GOI) — Annexure 1 (drugs dispensable by MLHP/nurse at SHC-HWC) and Annexure 2 (drugs indentable from PHC referral centre for chronic disease continuation). Teratogenic and injectable drugs are flagged — the Management Agent must check these against patient pregnancy status and clinical setting. This file is injected directly into Management Agent Call 2 prompts. Do not ingest into the vector store.

drugs.name: Paracetamol
drugs.notes: First-line antipyretic and analgesic

drugs.name: Diclofenac
drugs.notes: Avoid in pregnancy, renal impairment, peptic ulcer

drugs.name: Ibuprofen
drugs.notes: Avoid in pregnancy (esp. third trimester), renal impairment

drugs.name: Mefenamic acid
drugs.notes: Use short-term only; avoid in renal impairment

drugs.name: Acetylsalicylic acid (Aspirin)
drugs.notes: Avoid in children under 16 (Reye's syndrome), pregnancy, peptic ulcer

drugs.name: Albendazole
drugs.notes: Avoid in first trimester of pregnancy

drugs.name: Diethylcarbamazine (DEC)
drugs.notes: Used in lymphatic filariasis; avoid in pregnancy

drugs.name: Ciprofloxacin
drugs.notes: Avoid in pregnancy and children under 18 (cartilage risk). Oral forms for enteric/UTI. Topical for eye/ear infections

drugs.name: Amoxicillin
drugs.notes: Safe in pregnancy. First-line for ARI, otitis media, UTI in pregnancy

drugs.name: Metronidazole
drugs.notes: Avoid in first trimester. Use with caution in pregnancy (second/third trimester only if essential)

drugs.name: Gentamicin
drugs.notes: Teratogenic — avoid in pregnancy. Nephrotoxic and ototoxic. Reserve for serious infections only

drugs.name: Fluconazole
drugs.notes: Avoid in pregnancy (teratogenic at high doses)

drugs.name: Clotrimazole
drugs.notes: Topical only at this facility level

drugs.name: Chloroquine
drugs.notes: For P. vivax. Use with Primaquine (check G6PD). Safe in pregnancy for P. vivax treatment

drugs.name: Primaquine
drugs.notes: CONTRAINDICATED in pregnancy and G6PD deficiency. Radical cure for P. vivax and P. falciparum gametocyte clearance. Always check G6PD status before prescribing

drugs.name: Artesunate + Sulphadoxine-Pyrimethamine (AS+SP combi-pack)
forms: AS 25 mg + SP 250/12.5 mg (weight band: 5–<10 kg)
AS 50 mg + SP 500/25 mg (weight band: 10–<17 kg)
AS 100 mg + SP 750/37.5 mg (weight band: 17–<25 kg)
AS 150 mg + SP 500/25 mg x2 (weight band: 25–<36 kg)
AS 200 mg + SP 750/37.5 mg x2 (weight band: ≥36 kg)
drugs.notes: For P. falciparum (uncomplicated). CONTRAINDICATED in first trimester. Use with caution in second/third trimester — refer to MO. Artesunate injection is NOT available at SHC-HWC level — severe/complicated malaria must be referred

drugs.name: Ferrous salts
drugs.notes: Safe in pregnancy. Standard treatment for iron deficiency anaemia

drugs.name: Ferrous salt + Folic acid (IFA)
forms: Tablet 45 mg iron + 400 mcg folic acid (small IFA)
Tablet 100 mg iron + 500 mcg folic acid (large IFA)
Oral liquid 20 mg iron + 100 mcg folic acid/ml
drugs.notes: Recommended in pregnancy and adolescent girls. Large IFA for pregnant women

drugs.name: Folic acid
drugs.notes: Periconceptional and pregnancy use

drugs.name: Oral Rehydration Salts (ORS)
drugs.notes: First-line for diarrhoea and dehydration across all age groups including infants

drugs.name: Zinc sulphate
drugs.notes: Adjunct to ORS for diarrhoea in children under 5. 10 mg/day for infants <6 months, 20 mg/day for children ≥6 months. 14-day course

drugs.name: Povidone iodine
drugs.notes: Wound cleaning and antisepsis

drugs.name: Silver sulphadiazine
drugs.notes: Burns and wound infections

drugs.name: Vitamin A
drugs.notes: High-dose supplementation for children as per NHM protocol. Avoid high-dose in pregnancy (teratogenic)

drugs.name: Phytomenadione (Vitamin K1)
drugs.notes: Neonatal prophylaxis (IM at birth). Also for Vitamin K deficiency bleeding

drugs.name: Misoprostol
drugs.notes: Prevention of postpartum haemorrhage (600 mcg oral at delivery). CONTRAINDICATED in pregnancy for non-obstetric use. Requires MO oversight for obstetric use

drugs.name: Methylergometrine
drugs.notes: Management of postpartum haemorrhage. CONTRAINDICATED in pregnancy (before delivery). Requires MO oversight

drugs.name: Ethinylestradiol + Norethisterone (OCP)
drugs.notes: Combined oral contraceptive pill. Contraindicated in pregnancy, active hepatic disease, thromboembolic disorders

drugs.name: Levonorgestrel
drugs.notes: Emergency contraception — within 72 hours of unprotected intercourse

drugs.name: Condom (male)
forms: As per Drugs and Cosmetics Rules 1945 Schedule R standards

drugs.name: Copper IUD
drugs.notes: Requires trained MLHP for insertion; available at SHC-HWC level

drugs.name: Levonorgestrel IUD (hormonal)
drugs.notes: Insertion requires trained provider

drugs.name: Lignocaine
drugs.notes: Topical use only at SHC-HWC level (e.g. wound care, minor procedures)

drugs.name: Activated charcoal
drugs.notes: Acute poisoning — administer within 1 hour of ingestion if patient is conscious

drugs.name: Atropine
drugs.notes: Organophosphate poisoning; bradycardia. Emergency use only

drugs.name: Pralidoxime chloride (2-PAM)
drugs.notes: Organophosphate poisoning — use with Atropine. Emergency use only

drugs.name: Snake venom antiserum (polyvalent)
drugs.notes: Snakebite with systemic envenomation. Administer under MO supervision if possible; nurse may initiate in life-threatening envenomation. Refer after stabilisation

drugs.name: Adrenaline (Epinephrine)
drugs.notes: Anaphylaxis — IM injection (thigh) 0.01 mg/kg. Emergency use only

drugs.name: Hydrocortisone
drugs.notes: Anaphylaxis, adrenal crisis. Emergency use only

drugs.name: Dexamethasone
drugs.notes: Severe allergic reactions, cerebral oedema, croup. Emergency use only

drugs.name: Glyceryl trinitrate (GTN)
drugs.notes: Acute angina. Sublingual only. Contraindicated with PDE5 inhibitors

drugs.name: Oxygen (medicinal gas)
drugs.notes: Emergency respiratory support. Oxygen cylinder with trolley available at SHC-HWC

drugs.name: Amlodipine
drugs.notes: Continuation of established hypertension treatment. Initiation requires MO. Use with caution in pregnancy — refer

drugs.name: Atenolol
drugs.notes: Continuation only. Avoid abrupt withdrawal

drugs.name: Enalapril
drugs.notes: CONTRAINDICATED in pregnancy. Continuation only

drugs.name: Propranolol
drugs.notes: Continuation only. Avoid in asthma/COPD

drugs.name: Isosorbide dinitrate
drugs.notes: Continuation for angina. Initiation requires MO

drugs.name: Clopidogrel
drugs.notes: Continuation for post-MI/stroke patients. Initiation requires MO

drugs.name: Furosemide
drugs.notes: Continuation for heart failure / oedema. Monitor electrolytes. Avoid in dehydration

drugs.name: Hydrochlorothiazide
drugs.notes: Continuation for hypertension. Avoid in pregnancy

drugs.name: Spironolactone
drugs.notes: Continuation only. Avoid in pregnancy and hyperkalemia

drugs.name: Metformin
drugs.notes: Continuation for type 2 diabetes. Avoid in renal impairment, dehydration, contrast procedures

drugs.name: Glimepiride
drugs.notes: Continuation for type 2 diabetes. Risk of hypoglycaemia — counsel patient. Avoid in pregnancy

drugs.name: Insulin (Soluble, short-acting)
drugs.notes: Continuation for established insulin-dependent diabetes. Initiation and dose adjustment requires MO. Safe in pregnancy (preferred antidiabetic in pregnancy)

drugs.name: Insulin (Intermediate-acting NPH)
drugs.notes: Continuation only. MO to initiate and titrate

drugs.name: Insulin (Premix 30:70)
drugs.notes: Continuation only. MO to initiate and titrate

drugs.name: Carbamazepine
drugs.notes: Continuation for epilepsy. TERATOGENIC — counsel women of childbearing age. Do not stop abruptly. Initiation requires MO

drugs.name: Diazepam
drugs.notes: Continuation for epilepsy or anxiety. Avoid in pregnancy (first trimester; neonatal withdrawal). Oral form only at SHC-HWC

drugs.name: Phenobarbitone
drugs.notes: Continuation for epilepsy. TERATOGENIC. Do not stop abruptly. Initiation requires MO

drugs.name: Phenytoin
drugs.notes: TERATOGENIC. Injection for emergency seizure management only. Oral continuation requires MO oversight. Narrow therapeutic index — do not switch formulations

drugs.name: Sodium valproate
drugs.notes: HIGHLY TERATOGENIC — major neural tube defects and neurodevelopmental harm. Avoid in all women of childbearing age unless no alternative. Requires specialist oversight. Initiation never at SHC-HWC level

drugs.name: Salbutamol
drugs.notes: First-line reliever for asthma and COPD. Inhaled preferred. Nebulizer solution for acute exacerbations. Safe in pregnancy for asthma

not_available_at_shc_hwc: Oxytocin injection (postpartum haemorrhage — refer; Misoprostol/Methylergometrine oral available)
Magnesium sulphate injection (eclampsia — refer immediately; loading dose only if referral delayed and MO authorised)
Benzathine penicillin injection (rheumatic fever prophylaxis — PHC level)
Gentamicin injection for neonatal sepsis protocol (refer neonate)
Antituberculosis drugs (DOTS programme — PHC level, not dispensed at SHC-HWC independently)
IV fluids (Normal saline, Ringer's lactate, Dextrose) — refer or stabilise with oral ORS
